subject: [PATCH 11/11] mm/zsmalloc: Handle charge migration in zpdesc substitution

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with the new function zs_migrate_lruvec and suggested adding a check to ensure that the old and new lruvecs are not the same.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Agreed with David's suggestion and provided additional context on the potential issue.

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch series addresses charge migration in zsmalloc by handling zpdesc substitutions, which can change the node of compressed objects and their lruvec association. It introduces a new function zs_migrate_lruvec to transfer per-lruvec and node-level stats when a zspage replaces a zpdesc with one from a different NUMA node.

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with the new function zs_migrate_lruvec(), suggesting that it might not handle the case where the old and new zspages have different sizes.

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch series addresses charge migration in zsmalloc by handling zpdesc substitutions, which can change the node association of compressed objects. It introduces a new function to update per-lruvec and node-level stats when a zspage replaces a zpdesc from a different NUMA node.

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Identified a potential issue with the patch's handling of partial compressed objects and suggested adding additional checks to ensure correct behavior.

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Agreed with David's concerns and suggested a different approach to handle partial compressed objects, which would simplify the code and improve performance.

2026-03-17.patch_summary: This patch series addresses charge migration in zsmalloc by handling zpdesc substitutions, which may change the node of compressed objects and their lruvec association. It takes special care to address partial compressed objects at the beginning and end of swapped out zpdescs.
